content.full_text:
E & L Associates
Consulting Services
Motor Vehicle Specialists—

Elmer A. Brown
President

May 17, 1991


FPPC
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Legal Division

Dear Sir:

We have been requested by one of our clients to prepare a "State Campaign Contribution Limits" document similar to the attached federal statement.

The client has a broad base PAC based upon contributions from members. The members are corporations, companies and small mom and pop type operations. Some of the corporations and companies have their own PAC's based upon contributions from employees.

We would appreciate your response to the questions and any additional information you feel will assured our client will properly handle campaign donations. A statement regarding donations to initiatives and the different requirements, if any, for elections for statewide offices or state legislative races would also clarify current campaign donation requirements.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Elmer Brown

EB:md

attachments


2110 - K Street, Suite 22 • Sacramento, CA 95816                (916) 441-7129 • FAX (916) 441-7137

'91-05-17' 14:25 UNLIP PKG ASSOC

STATE
FEDERAL CAMPAIGN CONTRIBUTION LIMITS


How much may I give?
An individual may give up to $1000 per election (both primary
and general).
Husbands and wives are considered separate individuals;
therefore, a husband and wife may give up to $1000 a piece per
election (both primary and general).


Can I make a donation from my business checking account?
Yes, but only if the business is not incorporated. Corporate
contributions are expressly prohibited.


How much may PACs and groups give to a single candidate?
Groups which do not have political action committees (PACs)
may give up to $1000 per election (both primary and general).
PACs -- those multi-candidate committees which have been
registered with the Federal Elections Commission (FEC) for at least
6 months, received contributions from more than 50 donors, and made
contributions to over 5 candidates for federal office -- may give
up to $5000 per election (both primary and general).


Can I give a cash contribution?
Yes, but no cash contributions of over $99.99 can be accepted.


Is there anything else I should know?
o Checks should be made payable to:

o All contributions must be given to the campaign within five
days of receiving the donation.
o All contributions over $200 must include the name, address,
occupation and employer of the donor.
